final_draft:
Headline: Northbridge is now SOC 2 Type II certified

Body:
Northbridge is now SOC 2 Type II certified, confirming our data handling practices meet enterprise security standards.

This certification represents the completion of a comprehensive audit of our security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy controls. The audit evaluated not just our policies, but our actual operational practices over an extended period—the hallmark of SOC 2 Type II assessment.

For enterprise prospects currently evaluating our security posture during vendor review processes, this certification provides independent verification that we maintain the controls and safeguards expected in enterprise environments. For existing customers whose security teams have requested certification documentation, this addresses a common requirement in security questionnaires and procurement workflows.

SOC 2 Type II certification is one of the industry-standard credentials that enterprise organizations use to assess third-party vendors. By meeting this standard, we've demonstrated a commitment to the operational rigor and control discipline that enterprise customers depend on.

Customers and prospects who need the detailed audit report or certification documentation should reach out to their account team or contact our sales organization. The full scope of controls audited is available in the formal attestation report.

We recognize that security review processes are a routine part of enterprise procurement, and this certification is designed to streamline those conversations. It reflects our ongoing investment in the security infrastructure and practices that protect customer data and maintain the trust our users place in us.

Certification status, audit scope, and SLA terms vary by plan and are subject to change. Visit northbridgeapp.com/trust for current details.

final_review.notes: Strong execution. The draft communicates the key message clearly in the opening and reinforces it throughout. Voice is confident and factual without hyperbole. All banned words and phrases are avoided. No exclamation points present. The required disclaimer is included verbatim at the end. Reading level is appropriate—sentences are clear and jargon is either unavoidable (SOC 2 Type II, audit scope) or contextually necessary and well-explained. The draft avoids unqualified performance claims and competitor comparison. Tone matches the brief's requirement for "confident, factual, not celebratory." This is a clean pass.

violations.suggested_fix: Add the required disclaimer at the end of the email body (below the CTA, before the signature): 'Certification status, audit scope, and SLA terms vary by plan and are subject to change. Visit northbridgeapp.com/trust for current details.'

review.notes: The draft is well-written, maintains the key message clearly, and avoids banned words and jargon. However, the required disclaimer is missing. Since the draft references a specific security certification (SOC 2 Type II), the verbatim disclaimer must be included per the brand guide. This is the only violation."
